journal.name: Yale Journal of Law and Feminism

registry_refs.key: Yale Journal of Law and Feminism

publication_scope.notes: Recent issue metadata lives on YaleConnect; durable archive handoff is openyls.law.yale.edu; current files are Google Drive viewer links.

evidence.notes: Public /yjlf/home/ is allowed; /downloads/ and ajax_widget_new are disallowed.
Static HTML lists Volumes 37.1, 36.2, and 36.1 with author text and linked titles; links mix Google Drive, SharePoint, and OpenYLS.
Collection/item pages and core mapping/bitstream endpoints are permitted; crawl-delay 10 applies. Browse, search, OAI, and most REST endpoints are disallowed.
Redirects to collection e7601e5a-72ad-48cd-860c-479f2e0ac49d and returns an 827-byte DSpace Angular shell; item discovery requires sitemap or permitted mapping work.
Public viewer returned HTML and disclosed a drive.usercontent.google.com download URL; no PDF bytes were fetched.
Disallow: / for all user agents, so the discovered binary route was not requested.

reconnaissance.notes: Recent title/author/volume metadata is extractable, but file acquisition is blocked by the robots policy on the binary host and the durable DSpace collection is client-rendered.

include: Yale Journal of Law and Feminism print issues and articles
exclude: YaleConnect organization pages unrelated to publication content
scope.notes: Use the YaleConnect page only as a recent index and scope OpenYLS by collection UUID/handle.

entry_points.notes: Durable full-archive handoff, but rendered as a JS shell.

nodes.notes: Inline volume, author, and title list.
Durable item routes linked selectively from YaleConnect.
Google usercontent or OpenYLS bitstream; not validated here.

edges.notes: Title text is the anchor; author is the preceding text node in the same paragraph.
Journal heading links to the durable collection.

edges.locator: OpenYLS collection sitemap or permitted core mapping route
edges.notes: Collection HTML is a JS shell; do not use disallowed browse/search/OAI endpoints.
Discovered but robots-disallowed; do not fetch in the crawler.

archive_patterns.notes: Current viewer route; not a durable metadata record.
Observed selective item link; systematic discovery unresolved.

pagination.termination: Angular shell does not expose pagination server-side.
pagination.notes: Use only robots-permitted sitemap/mapping routes in a follow-up.

layout_eras.label: YaleConnect hand-authored recent issue list
layout_eras.notes: Three visible issues; markup is irregular and authors are text nodes.

layout_eras.label: OpenYLS DSpace backfile
layout_eras.notes: Full date range and item layout not sampled.

sources.locator: text node immediately preceding each linked title within .text-left or .cgLead p
sources.notes: No dedicated author element; split on preceding br and comma.

abstract.missingness: Not exposed on YaleConnect and no OpenYLS item was sampled.

sources.locator: OpenYLS item metadata or PDF citation

sources.notes: Volume label also embeds issue after decimal.

normalization: Parse Volume N.M into volume N
Parse Volume N.M into issue M

sources.notes: Only some legacy empty anchors embed page ranges.

pages.missingness: Not present for most visible titles.

sources.locator: volume label + author text + linked title
sources.example: Emily Suski, Title IX's Post-Skrmetti Peril & Promise, Vol. 37.1

patterns.notes: Viewer is public HTML, but its binary usercontent host disallows all crawling.
Potential compliant alternative; item-to-bitstream mapping has not been established.

validation.notes: Viewer returned text/html; binary smoke was intentionally not attempted because drive.usercontent.google.com disallows /.

robots.notes: Crawl-delay 10; collection/items and core mapping/bitstream routes permitted, browse/search/OAI and most REST routes disallowed.

barriers.notes: Current Google Drive binary route cannot be fetched by the crawler.
OpenYLS collection page is an Angular shell.

access.politeness_notes: Robots checked on every newly discovered host; OpenYLS crawl-delay 10 honored; stopped before robots-disallowed binary request.

verdict.summary: YaleConnect yields recent volume, issue, title, and fragile author metadata, while current binary delivery is robots-disallowed and the durable OpenYLS archive needs a compliant sitemap/mapping implementation.
blockers: Recent index omits year, abstract, DOI, and usually pages
verdict.next_action: Build a robots-compliant OpenYLS sitemap/core-mapping probe with Crawl-delay 10 and use it to map durable item metadata and bitstreams; do not crawl Google usercontent.
verdict.implementation_notes: Parse recent authors from preceding text nodes, ignore empty SharePoint anchors, and prefer durable OpenYLS records once mapped.
